hook.text: Matthew Maury created the first ocean wind and current charts in the 1850s.

buildup.text: He analyzed thousands of ship logbooks to map patterns no single sailor could see.

discovery.text: His charts cut Atlantic crossing times by weeks, saving ships and lives.

twist.text: The ocean floor remained unmapped until sonar revealed mountains and trenches in the 1950s.

climax.text: Marie Tharp mapped the Mid-Atlantic Ridge, proving continental drift with a pencil.

punchline.text: One woman drew the map that moved continents.

quiz.question: Who mapped the Mid-Atlantic Ridge from sonar data?
